name: Cusine D'or Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter, slightly cooler than room temperature
1 3/4 cups (minus 1 1/2 tablespoons) Brown sugar, firmly packed
2 large Eggs, at room temperature, lightly beaten
8 ounces Swiss milk chocolate, chopped into small pieces
8 ounces Swiss semisweet chocolate, chopped into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour with baking soda and salt.
2. Set aside.
3. With an electric mixer, cream the butter until softened.
4. Add brown sugar; cream until light and fluffy. Scrape down the bowl often.
5. Blend in corn syrup, lightly beaten eggs, vanilla extract, and almond extract. If the mixture seems curdled or uneven, add a bit of the flour mixture to bind it.
6. Stir dry ingredients into the creamed mixture, scraping down sides of the bowl occasionally.
7. Gently stir in chocolate pieces by hand.
8. Chill batter for 20 minutes.
9. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
10. Drop rounded teaspoonfuls onto parchment-lined baking sheets and flatten cookies slightly.
11. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 12 minutes, or until cookies are lightly golden brown.
12. Cool 1 minute on pans, then remove to cooling rack.
13. Store in the refrigerator to preserve flavors.
Yield: 4 dozen.
